expectations.text: Output contains an explicit rating on a defined scale
expectations.evidence: 'Note : 3 -- Maybe' with full 5-point scale table and explanation

expectations.text: Strengths section with specific evidence from profile
expectations.evidence: 5 strengths grounded in profile (Criteo/Datadog environments, progression, Datadog recruitment involvement, international context)

expectations.text: Gaps section distinguishing unknowns from concerns
expectations.evidence: 5 gaps labeled as 'Inconnu (a explorer en priorite)', 'Inconnu', or 'Mineur'
4 risks including 'tech lead deguise en manager', perf management urgency, scope increase, and identity loss
Full framework: 'Si vous prenez cet appel...', 'bon fit si...', 'pas un fit si...', 'Ce qui changerait votre avis...'

expectations.text: Interview questions use behavioral format targeting perf management and conflict
expectations.evidence: 7 STAR questions. Q1 directly targets perf management, Q3 targets conflict, Q7 targets difficult feedback

expectations.text: Includes a scoring rubric for the call
expectations.evidence: 4-dimension rubric with Signal fort/Acceptable/Signal faible columns
Q1: perf management, Q2: code vs manage time split, Q3: conflict resolution, Q5: tech lead delegation, Q6: management reality check

expectations.text: The entire output is in French
expectations.evidence: 100% French throughout — headings, body text, questions, rubric, everything
